topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used in System and Network administration, DevOps, Pentesting, and Security Research.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers, but it can be useful for anyone.

entries.q: What type of content can be found in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The repository contains a collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: How can users contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can contribute to the repository by making pull requests, adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about their changes or comments, and following the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What is the license of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The repository is licensed under the MIT License.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI tools included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of CLI tools included in the repository are GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, Oh My ZSH!, and Oh My Fish.

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of shell plugins included in the repository are z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, and zsh-syntax-highlighting.

entries.q: How can users stay updated with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can stay updated with changes to the repository by using the RSS/Atom feed of the commits.
